en: This requirement is triggered because you indicated your organization maintains a valid Japan Construction Business License in the Japan Construction Business License Applicability Assessment questionnaire.

The Japan Construction Business License is a statutory authorization established under the Construction Business Act that certifies a company’s legal eligibility to undertake construction work in Japan. It serves to regulate construction contractors and ensure that licensed firms meet the required financial, technical, and compliance standards, including those related to occupational health and safety and facility operation. The license is mandatory for businesses performing construction work exceeding specified contract thresholds, except in certain exempt cases. Its primary purpose is to maintain public safety, construction quality, and administrative integrity within the industry. Construction Business Act (Law No. 100)
